design: Same-county CDL-verified corn patches; production detectDamage(); stress season (2023-06-20, documented IL flash drought) vs control (2021-06-20, near-normal). Patch locations from extract-features.ts (deterministic).
limitations: Patch-level, not farmer-field-level; ~25-acre rectangles, not real boundaries.
No per-field ground-truth labels exist yet — this tests discrimination between a documented stress season and a quiet one, not absolute accuracy.
2023 drought severity varied across IL; some sampled counties were less affected, so <100% fire rate in 2023 is expected and honest.
Corroborating public record: USDA reported drought as the dominant 2023 IL cause of loss (RMA cause-of-loss files) — regional consistency, not field validation.

interpretation.headline: The engine did NOT discriminate at patch level in this design: 0/8 fired in the 2023 drought season, 2/8 fired in the 2021 'control'. Reported as-is.
readings: Severity magnitudes moved the right way in 2023 (Boone 42, DeKalb 40, Henry 43, Bureau 33 — vs the single-field proof's 62 on a real DeKalb boundary), but crossed the significance gate in none — the gate is conservative for region-wide events on thin/contaminated baselines.

Likely baseline contamination: patches were selected as corn IN THE EVENT YEAR; under IL corn/soy rotation many were soybeans in baseline years, so the 'field's own history' the engine compares against is a different crop with a different mid-June NDVI — depressing the expected value and hiding the drought drop. Real farmer fields carry crop-season records; random patches don't.

2021 was not a clean control in far-north IL: Boone/Henry (the two 2021 fires) sat in the real June-2021 northern-Illinois dry spell (USDM D1-D2). The 'false positives' may be true positives against a mislabeled control.

The earlier single-field proof (Home 80, DeKalb, real boundary, 4 scanned seasons) detected the same 2023 event: significant=true, severity 62%, confidence 0.95 — the engine performs on the data shape it was designed for.
actions: Do not tune detection thresholds to make this backtest pass (that is overfitting to the test and would require a methodology version bump).
Re-run with rotation-consistent patches: require the SAME crop in event AND baseline years via CDL (methodology-neutral change to the test, not the engine).
Pick a cleaner control (2021 central/southern IL only, or 2020).
The definitive measurement remains field-level adjuster-graded labels from the flywheel.
